diagrams.title: Integrity Constraint Types Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows three main integrity constraint types and their sub-categories
elements: Domain Integrity (CHECK, DEFAULT, Data Types, User-defined)
diagrams.purpose: Helps visualize how different constraints are categorized and their relationships to each other and to database tables

diagrams.title: Primary Key-Foreign Key Relationship
diagrams.description: Illustrates parent-child table relationship with referential integrity
elements: Child Table: Employee (EmpID PK, Name, DeptID FK)
Arrow from Employee.DeptID pointing to Department.DeptID
Shows CASCADE, SET NULL, RESTRICT options
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how foreign key establishes connection between tables and what happens during update/delete operations

diagrams.title: Candidate Key Selection Process
diagrams.description: Shows how candidate keys are identified and one is chosen as primary key
elements: All superkeys identified (StudentID, Email, Phone, etc.)
Minimal superkeys highlighted as candidate keys
diagrams.purpose: Helps understand the relationship between superkeys, candidate keys, and primary keys
